text: When working on a software project with a team, version control is essential, and Git is a popular tool for this. A common workflow involves creating feature branches. These are separate copies of the main code where you can develop new features or fix bugs without affecting the stable version. Once your work on a feature branch is complete, you can create a pull request. This allows other team members to review your code before it is added to the main branch. After approval, the feature branch is merged into the main codebase. This organised process helps teams collaborate efficiently while keeping the project safe and well-managed.
